feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Collaborative draft
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the singleton pattern is used when generating the draft enabled RAP BO. One characteristic of this pattern is that all child entities are locked together, so it is not possible for multiple users to work with the same configuration at the same time.

This blog describes how to avoid this restriction by using collaborative draft.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (2025)
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Enable collaborative draft

In the generated behavior definition, add "collaborative" to the draft annotation:

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_COMPANYCODE_S unique;
strict;
with collaborative draft;

Add the draft query annotation and the share action to the singleton entity definition:

define behavior for ZI_CompanyCode_S alias CompanyCodeAll
draft table ZDEMO_CC_D_S query ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
[...]
{
  draft action Share;
  [...]
}

 Right-click the singleton draft table and choose New Data Definition to create the CDS view for the draft query:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Draft Query View'
define view entity ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
  as select from zdemo_cc_d_s
{
  key singletonid                   as Singletonid,
      lastchangedatmax              as Lastchangedatmax,
      transportrequestid            as Transportrequestid,
      draftentitycreationdatetime   as Draftentitycreationdatetime,
      draftentitylastchangedatetime as Draftentitylastchangedatetime,
      draftadministrativedatauuid   as Draftadministrativedatauuid,
      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

You need to enhance the authorization of the involved users. In SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition you assign business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_RAP_DRAFT_PC to the user, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition authorization object S_DRAFT.

Collaboration

User TB is creating a new company code 400 while at the same time user PW is editing company code 100. All changes are recorded on the same transport request.

Since the collaborative draft is enabled for the singleton root entity, the save and discard draft action are applied to all object changes. In this example, users PW and TB process two different company codes, which are logically different objects. Technically, however, they process the same singleton root entity. In practice, this means that discarding the draft or saving must be aligned between the users involved, as the save action persists the changes in all company codes.
For completely independent editing of individual objects, you must develop a RAP BO without a singleton pattern.

entry.title: Spot Tender in SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean Service based Settlement. (Default Charge Type)
entry.content:
This blog post will help readers, customers, and SAP TM users familiarize themselves with the SAP Transportation Management’s Spot procurement functionality in SAP S/4 HANA 2025 with Lean Service based Transportation Settlement.

SAP Transportation Management’s spot procurement is a subset of the Tendering process having 2 broad categories i.e. Contracted and non-contracted carrier.

In my previous blog post I highlighted step-by-step process for one of the many ways in which we can carry out spot tender in version below S/4 HANA TM 2025. Going forward the process has evolved compared to previous version and also based on the business needs and processes.

Below is the link to PART 1 of Spot Tender which clearing calls out detailed process steps:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-members/spot-tender-in-sap-s-4hana-transportation-management/ba-p/13541617

You can read more about SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management -Tendering in the Application Help for SAP S/4HANA
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/c336c907db3742eeb97e9138dd419970.html?locale=en-US

Note: I have carried out the demonstration with relevant screen shot from an internal system for ease of understanding. There’s no relevance or relation to any external source.

Use Case

Use case is for both the customers in Transportation & Logistics Industry (T&L) as well as shippers from Retail, CPG, manufactures etc. who procure services to move their loads or are in business of providing transportation services and have implemented SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean service procurement.

Assumption: 

The customer is a CPG company and implemented SAP S/4HANA 2025 SAP Transportation Management along with Lean service procurement.

They have yearly contracts with the carriers to move certain capacities on a given corridor/ route. Alternatively, they may also reach out to carriers who don’t have a contract. Contract in the SAP TM world should be referred as FRA (Freight Agreement).

Challenge:

During certain festive seasons the demand on a particular corridor may outweigh the capacity already procured during the start of the year. In this situation, it becomes really very important to meet the increasing demand and ship the goods to our esteemed customers within the stipulated time frame.

Towards the end of the year the procurement team along with Finance would like to analyze the premium paid as transportation services and how it can be curtailed in the upcoming fiscal year. Alternatively, For many years clients have been requesting to book premium cost in a different G/L and replace the std ADJ charge into a more business defined terminology.

In an ideal world, in the absence of the above the cost segregation is cumbersome and involves additional resources and efforts which aren’t quantified. For the leadership as well, this seems to be a non-transparent process with certainly being a  post facto process to adjust the book of records.

Solution:

SAP S/4 HANA TM 2025+ with Lean Service procurement has come out with an out of the box provision to modify the std ADJ charge type during spot tender process which provides step-by-step visibility and more opportunity to drive the tender process both from process as well as financial stand point. (*some limitations and observations shared in the trail blog post)

Scenario:

Let’s move directly at the stage where a FRO is created and subcontracted. Further, we have received a response for an RFQ from the carrier with evaluation completed. We will begin from the step where the user has decided to manually award the carrier.

We start with an assumption that all Tender relevant configs are in place, as the focus is not on setting up the system but on functionality walk-through.

Pictorial representation of the demonstrated functionality:

Basic Tender flow (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Note: Let us now consider two scenarios in the case of Award quotation:

  1. Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
  2. Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Scenario 1: Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
 
Step 1: User is trying to award a carrier and stumbled upon below messages.

The system has hindered the carrier award process and will not allow to proceed further.

Let us analyze how to can overcome the errors.

Step 2: In order to resolve above error, we can be leverage below BADI and implementation as showcased below.

BADI: /SCMTMS/TCC_TEND_DFLT_CHRG_TYP

Here you can observe that I have created a new charge type ZADITYA to replace ADJ. (Charge type created for representation purpose only)

The glaring difference compared to previous blog/version is that system now restricts and aborts the award process which wasn’t the case earlier.

Step 3: Let us try to award the carrier Post BADI implementation.

The popped messages don’t hinder with the carrier award process and carrier is successfully awarded.

Step 4: When we observe the Charges tab, awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and system has assigned the entire spot tender amount i.e. 4500 USD earlier viewed under Adjusted/Quoted Amount charge type to ZADITYA.

You can further map the Charge type to different G/L to apportion the cost accordingly. (* Not part of this blog Post)

Now let us look at the Flip side i.e. Awarding Carrier having an FRA (Freight Agreement)

Scenario 2:  Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Note: The BADI implementation is still active.

Step 5:Post awarding the carrier, since system determined an FRA, it displayed below messages with no visible warnings nor errors.

Now let’s Analyze the Charge Tab.

Step 6: In this case, the awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and has an existing agreement with charges amounting to 2000 USD. System subtracts agreement charges from the submitted charges i.e. giving a clear picture of the individual charges incurred.

The glaring difference to note in contacted scenario is that system didn’t replace the ADJ charge type but continues to work as previous version.

You will have to continue to apportion premium charges as part of ADJ charge type with 2 different ways of handling the process for the customer having both active scenarios.

*Will share more insights but at this stage the above is the observed system behavior.

The demonstration in this blog post doesn’t focus on the configs to setup the system nor does it highlight the further freight settlement process.

Hope this article helps all of us understand the upcoming new functionality of the spot tender process in the SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2025 with Lean service procurement. There may be multiple different ways to carry out the tendering process but, in this demonstration, I chose to keep it generic and simple.

Thank you for taking time and effort to read this.

Since the tests are carried out with limited resources and purely based on observations, any Feedback/comments in any form will be highly appreciated.

SAP Cloud Transport Management SAP Transportation Management Travel and Transportation SAP S/4HANA SAP Business Network for Logistics

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-26 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
As we continue into 2026, I’m back with the next edition of my quarterly blog series covering the latest innovations and highlights in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Over the last quarter, we’ve extended existing capabilities, introduced new ones, and invested further in usability, integration, and enablement – all with the goal of helping our customers calculate, analyze, and operationalize sustainability footprints at scale, grounded in ERP data. In this edition, I’m covering:

  • Transport Footprint Enhancements
  • Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)
  • Distance Adjustment Factors
  • Transport Analytics
  • Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints
  • Usability Improvements
  • Other Release Highlights
  • Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets
  • Multi‑Impact Footprints in Practice: Learning Journey, FLAG and TfS Use Cases
  • Additional Information

Transport Footprint Enhancements

The last quarter brought several enhancements to the ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation capabilities in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. These updates extend coverage to customer-relevant transport footprints, including integration of customer master data, give you more control over the distance calculations used for transport footprints, and provide you with comprehensive analytical capabilities.

Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

In one of my previous posts, I introduced ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation for upstream and internal transport, covering Greenhouse Gas Protocol Scope 3.4 emissions. This capability has now been extended to the downstream side of the value chain, enabling the calculation of customer‑relevant transport footprints and, with that, Scope 3.9 (Downstream Transport and Distribution) emissions.

The calculation also leverages existing ERP business transaction data and builds on the same transport modeling approach used for upstream transport. The foundation of this enhancement is the introduction of a Customer role for business partners. Business partners with this role, including address data, can be replicated directly from integrated SAP S/4HANA source systems, through APIs, or imported via file in the Import Master Data app. The system derives customer‑relevant transport activities from the following business transactions:

  • Goods Issue for Customer (Outbound deliveries of products to customers)
  • Customer Return (Goods returned by customers to company locations).

When business transactions are replicated from integrated source systems, these transactions now include the Customer ID, ensuring correct assignment of customer‑relevant transport activities during transport footprint calculation. In this context, targeted enhancements were made to the Import Master Data and Import Business Transactions OData APIs on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Based on the configurations in the Manage Transport Settings app, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management automatically generates transport activities, determines routes and legs, and calculates footprints per activity, route, and leg, applying the relevant emission factors by mode of transport. The resulting Scope 3.9 emissions are seamlessly integrated into both Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) and Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculations and are available in the standard footprint result and analytical apps – all grounded in ERP data rather than manual estimates.

For setup and process details, see Calculating Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints Using Business Transactions in SAP Help. For importing business partners and business transactions, check Configurations for Importing Master Data and Configurations for Importing Business Transaction Data.

Customer Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport footprint calculations in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management derive distances between locations automatically using the Great Circle Distance (GCD) method. Since real transport routes often deviate from the shortest path due to road networks, infrastructure, and routing constraints, GCD can underestimate the actual distance traveled. To reflect this, you can now configure distance adjustment factors for different modes of transport in the Manage Transport Settings app. When routes and legs are generated, the system applies the configured adjustment factor to the GCD, resulting in a more realistic distance basis for footprint calculations.

Before configuring distance adjustment factors, you should review the methodology documentation of your emission factor sources. Some emission factor datasets already include distance adjustments – for example, GLEC v3.1 emission factors for air transport. Applying an additional adjustment factor in such cases would lead to incorrect results.

Also note that existing transport activities aren’t updated automatically. To apply new distance adjustment factors, routes and legs must be regenerated and the relevant transport footprints recalculated. For details, check SAP Help: Configuring Distance Adjustment Factors and Generating Routes and Legs.

Configure Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport Analytics

To complement the transport footprint calculation capabilities, a new analytics app called Transport is now available in the Analytics section of the SAP Fiori launchpad. The app provides a comprehensive, map‑based view of corporate and product transport footprints for a selected footprint inventory scope and period. Transport legs are visualized as connections between source and target locations, with color‑coding that highlights higher‑emission legs. You can filter by product, mode of transport, business transaction type, or location, and switch to a table view for detailed, leg‑level analysis sorted by emission value. The app also supports comparisons across different periods. To use the app, you need the SUS_PFM_Calculate_Transport_Footprints_Read role. See SAP Help for more information: Analyzing Footprints for Transport.

Transport Analytics

To reflect all those updates, the process steps for calculating transport footprints using business transaction data have also been updated in the Scenario Guide: Process 8 – Calculating Scope 3.4 and Scope 3.9 Emissions Using Business Transaction Data.

 

Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints

Until now, all purchased product footprints covered in the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app were automatically attributed to GHG Protocol Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) in corporate carbon footprint calculations. For most companies, this is the correct assignment – but not in all cases. Capital goods such as machinery, equipment, or buildings that are purchased and used over multiple years belong to Scope 3.2 (Capital Goods). Correctly distinguishing between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2 is important both for GHG Protocol compliance and for maintaining a high‑quality footprint inventory. This is also a feature that was requested by customers via SAP Influence.

With a recent enhancement, you can now explicitly assign a purchased product footprint or product group footprint to either:

  • Scope 3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services, or
  • Scope 3.2 – Capital Goods

This categorization is supported consistently across all relevant workflows in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management:

  • When creating purchased product footprints manually,
  • When mapping emission factors manually or using AI‑based mapping, and
  • When importing purchased product footprints via data files.

Scope 3.2 Categorization

This enhancement ensures that each purchased product footprint is correctly attributed when calculating your organizational footprint inventory, resulting in a more accurate and auditable split between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2. Existing purchased product footprints that were previously categorized automatically remain assigned to Scope 3.1 by default, ensuring backward compatibility. For more information, see Creating Footprints for Purchased Products and Product Groups in SAP Help.

For analyzing your calculated footprints, the updated categorization is also reflected correctly in the Footprint Overview app, where purchased product footprints assigned to Scope 3.2 are shown separately from Scope 3.1.

 

Usability Improvements

To support more efficient day‑to‑day work with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we continuously invest in improving usability and increasing automation. Last quarter, we introduced several enhancements around form‑based data collection, manual data imports, and better transparency in integrations. These improvements focus on reducing manual effort in recurring data collection tasks, simplifying bulk operations, and making changes and corrections easier to manage at scale – especially in scenarios with large numbers of forms, users, or integrated systems. Many of these enhancements are the direct result of customer feedback, collected through SAP Influence as well as ongoing conversations with customers and partners.

You can now change the status of GHG data forms directly during data import when using spreadsheet templates in the Record GHG Data and Manage GHG Data Collections apps. GHG forms can be set to Applicable or Not Applicable as part of the import. When setting a form to Not Applicable, a comment is required, consistent with the behavior in the user interface. This applies to both bulk templates on the main page of the apps and the individual templates per GHG form type, streamlining bulk updates and reducing manual follow‑up steps.

To further automate and improve correction workflows, GHG data forms that were previously set to Completed or Not Applicable can now be reopened via API using the POST/massUpdate action in the GHG Data Forms API on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This provides additional flexibility for automated correction scenarios and integration workflows where reported data needs to be revised after submission.

For providing a more consistent and efficient user experience, downloaded templates now better reflect the UI and applied filters. Template sorting now mirrors the UI table sorting, and templates can be downloaded together with the currently applied UI filters. These enhancements apply to apps such as Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates, Manage Business Activities, Model Energy Flows, Manage Waste Footprints, Record GHG Data, Manage Transport Operation Footprints, Manage Allocations, Manage Purchased Product Footprints, and Manage Transport Settings. In the Calculate Footprints, Model Energy Flows, Record GHG Data, and Manage GHG Data Collections apps, downloading templates together with the displayed data is now the default behavior.

Finally, the sustainability data integration apps now include a Change History tab in Manage Replications, Manage Source Systems, and Configure Extensions for Replications. This allows you to track who made which changes and when, including the old and new values for edited fields, improving transparency and traceability when managing replication configurations.

 

Other Release Highlights

When performing a current period calculation, input items for footprint items of type Product that only contribute a defined CO₂e value without a product quantity are no longer considered in the per‑unit calculation. These input items are excluded because they have no associated product quantity and would otherwise distort per‑unit results, for example transports or allocations. Their CO₂e values are still fully aggregated into the total footprint. When using CO₂e‑only input items, make sure they’re scaled to the total product quantity where appropriate.

The system now supports goods receipt from production even without production history such as goods issues to production, work‑in‑progress postings, or internal service confirmations in the current period. In these cases, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management uses the previous period footprint per unit of the produced product or, if not available, maintained purchased product footprints. This eliminates previous calculation errors and applies the product footprint determination methods defined in the calculation variant.

Custom temperature conditions can now be replicated from source systems using the Manage Replications app. This applies to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition source systems. For pull‑based replication scenarios, the setup requires custom CDS views in the source system.

Lastly, we’re also continuously enhancing our documentation on SAP Help to improve transparency and onboarding. The Implement – Getting Started section now includes a link to ERP Objects, providing details on relevant CDS views, tables, and fields in SAP S/4HANA for each supported master data entity. In addition, the Product Carbon Footprint Scenario Guide has been extended with step‑by‑step processes for calculating product carbon footprints for cradle‑to‑gate and cradle‑to‑grave scenarios, including concrete examples for allocating Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions using business activities, allocations, and business transaction data.

 

Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets

One of the persistent challenges in Scope 3 reporting is the gap between supplier‑specific data and the global average emission factors that many companies fall back on when that data isn’t available. Those averages can be misleading. For agricultural commodities, for instance, emission factors can vary by up to 200% depending on the country of origin, reflecting differences in land‑use change, energy consumption, and local production practices. The wider the gap between your actual supply chain and the averages you apply, the less reliable your footprint calculations become. To overcome this challenge, we onboarded a new addition to the SAP partner ecosystem: Unibloom. Unibloom’s country‑specific supply‑chain emissions datasets are now available on SAP Store and can be integrated directly with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

Unibloom provides ISO 14044‑aligned, country‑specific emission factors for agricultural and industrial commodities, including land‑use‑ and management‑adjusted data. The coverage spans key sectors such as SBTi FLAG (Forest, Land, and Agriculture). The datasets are delivered in a ready‑to‑import format and can be extended on demand with additional country‑specific factors for specific materials, ingredients, or industries. With Unibloom datasets in place, you can replace broad global averages with granular, location‑specific emission factors in your footprint calculations. This improves accuracy and auditability, increases confidence in reported results, and supports more informed sourcing and procurement decisions.

To go deeper into the datasets and their practical use with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we’re hosting a joint webinar with Unibloom. The session focuses on improving Scope 3 accuracy for agricultural and FLAG‑relevant value chains and shows how country‑specific emission factors can be applied in practice. Access the webinar and register here: End data paralysis. Know when your Scope 3 data is good enough.

 

Multi‑Impact Footprints in Practice: Learning Journey, FLAG and TfS Use Cases

To support our customers in moving beyond pure carbon metrics, we introduced multi‑impact environmental footprints in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management last quarter – I covered the core capability in more detail in my deep dive.

To help you apply this capability in practice, consistently across different reporting frameworks and industry requirements, we’ve invested in further enablement resources. As a starting point, a new Learning Journey provides a structured introduction to the topic: Establishing the Multi-impact Environmental Footprint with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. It walks through the underlying concepts, configuration, and usage of multi‑impact footprints, providing the context for how additional environmental impact categories can be calculated, managed, and analyzed alongside carbon, using a single, consistent data model.

With the basics in place, the focus is now shifting from how multi‑impact categories work to how they can be applied in practice for concrete reporting frameworks and industry requirements. In this context, my colleague @Cielo_SanabriaRuiz has published two follow‑up posts that demonstrate exactly that:

The first, SBTi Forest, Land, and Agriculture (FLAG) in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, explains why distinguishing land‑based emissions from fossil emissions matters at both corporate and product level. It walks through the specific impact categories and emission factors required for SBTi FLAG compliance and includes a mapping table that links impact categories to FLAG reporting categories.

The second, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for the Chemicals (Together for Sustainability), focuses on the chemical industry and the Together for Sustainability (TfS) guideline. It covers the sector‑specific setup for product carbon footprints and Scope 3.1 calculations, including allocation rules for multi‑output processes, transport requirements such as the Radiative Forcing Index (RFI) for air transport, and a complete mapping of impact categories to TfS reporting standards.

Taken together, this enablement material shows how multi‑impact footprints can be used to support FLAG and TfS reporting requirements without fragmenting data models or calculation logic, while still staying aligned with established standards. If you’re working on FLAG reporting, chemical‑industry footprinting, or more broadly on extending footprint calculations beyond carbon, these resources provide a coherent path from concepts to practical application.

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release. To continue receiving e-mail notifications, you need to subscribe again – just visit the page and choose Subscribe.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-day trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through all the features and functions comprehensively. Recently, we also added new tours and will shortly release additional ones, representing the latest features and an enhanced user experience.

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (004) - SAPカーネルアップデート
entry.content:
SAPカーネルはOSやデータベースに依存するプログラム群であり、脆弱性対応などの理由から比較的頻繁にアップデートが行われるコンポーネントの一つです。なお、SAPカーネルはSAPシステムにとって非常に重要なコンポネントであるため、SAPカーネルアップデート後は、お客様側にてビジネスシナリオのテストを実施することを推奨します。

本記事では、SAPカーネルをアップデートをRISE側に依頼する際にご利用するSRテンプレートについてご紹介します。
また、スクリーンショットは2026/04/10のものとなりますので、ご了承願います。

利用するSRテンプレート

SAPカーネルアップデートを依頼する際、ご利用するSRテンプレートは下記の2つとなります。

  • SAPカーネルアップデート: Update SAP Kernel
  • SAPカーネルアップデート(Hotfix含む): Update SAP Kernel (Expert mode - including hotfix)

なお、選択されたシステム(S/4HANA APサーバ、Web Dispatcherなど)とオプションによってSRテンプレートでの表示内容が変わりますので、ご注意願います。

SAPカーネルアップデート

下記はS/4HANAのAPサーバのSAPカーネルアップデートを依頼する際のスクリーンショットとなります。

Kernel Release: リストからアップデート対象のSAPカーネルバージョンを選択する
Kernel SP Stack Number: リストからアップデート対象のSAPカーネルバージョンのSP(※1)を選択する
IGS Release: IGS(Internet Graphics Service)のバージョンアップは必須ではありませんので、必要に応じて選択してください。
IGS Patch Number: IGS Releaseで選択されたPatch Numberが表示されます。
Set system message (for ABAP systems): ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。
Target HANA Client Version: HANA Clientバージョンのアップデートは必須ではありませんので、必要に応じて選択してください。アップデートを実施しない場合は、Optoutを選択してください。
※1: SP(Service Pack)はPL(Patch Level)とも記載されるケースがございます。同じものとなります。

 

SAPカーネルアップデート(Hotfix含む)

Hotfixを適用する場合、環境間での整合性を維持するため、最初に開発環境のアップデートを実施し、品質環境と本番環境での適用を実施する際には開発環境を参照環境として(オプションProvide HOTFIX Patch Details: Same level as in Reference System)指定することを推奨します。

まずは、開発環境のS/4HANA APサーバのSAPカーネルアップデート(Hotfix含む)を依頼する際のスクリーンショットとなります。

  • Options: ALL、HOTFIX、ALL(MANUAL)の3つオプションがございますが、ほとんどのケースではHOTFIXオプションで要件がみたされます。各オプションの詳細は「?」をクリックして詳細をご確認ください。
  • Current Version: 現状ご利用のバージョン情報が表示されます。
  • Provide HOTFIX Patch Details (Refer Help Text): 初めてのシステム(通常は開発環境)は、リストから"Latest patch available in SAP Software Downloads"を選択してください。
  • Apply the latest hotfix to just one system in the landscape for uniformity. See help text.: チェックを入れてください。
  • Select ONLY the relevant HOTFIX component/s (Refer Help Text): リストからHotfixを適用するコンポネントを選択してください。この例ではdwのみを選択しております。
  • Set system message (for ABAP systems): ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。
  • NOTE: Create service request for Kernel Update in sequence (DEV->QA->PRD) to reduce PRD system risks: お客様によっては、本番環境から適用していくケース(推奨ではない)もございますが、必須項目となるため、チェックを入れてください。

 

 

下記は品質環境と本番環境の例となります。開発環境との主な違いは下記2つのオプションとなります。

  • Provide HOTFIX Patch Details (Refer Help Text): リストから"Same level as in Reference System"を選択することで、SAPカーネルバージョン(Hotfix含む)を参照システムと同じバージョンにします。
  • Reference System Number: リストから参照対象システムを選択します。ランドスケープにて最初にSAPカーネルアップデートを実施したシステムを指定します。

 

参照SAP Notes

2083594 - SAP Kernel Versions and SAP Kernel Patch Levels 
2907361 - Release Roadmap for Kernel 77x, 78x, 793 and 916 
3628821 - FAQ on Patching SAP Kernel 
3156085 - How to check IGS version

entry.title: How to get started with the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench
entry.content:
Now a days, companies are actively exploring easier and more reliable methods to upgrade and migrate their systems to unlock the full value of SAP S/4HANA and cloud solutions. 

Many organizations want a simplified and guided approach that reduces complexity during system transitions. Transition projects often involve multiple technical steps, tools, and dependencies that must be carefully managed. 

The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench helps safeguard transition projects by providing a single centralized entry point. It offers a highly automated and guided system transition procedure designed to streamline the entire process. The workbench combines all necessary technical activities and tools into one structured framework. This approach reduces manual effort and minimizes the risk of errors during system upgrades and migrations. 

By integrating different SAP software logistics tools, the workbench simplifies the overall transition experience. As a result, customers can move to SAP S/4HANA and the cloud more efficiently while ensuring a smooth and reliable transformation journey. 

This blog covers:  

  1. What is “Rise with SAP System Transition Workbench”? 
  2. Available approaches 
  3. Preparation activities on SAP ECS and customer side 
  4. How to access the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" to run cockpits 
  5. Advantages 
  6. Usage restriction 
  7. Frequently asked questions 
  8. References  

What is the "RISE with SAP system transition workbench"? 

  • The "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" is the single-entry point for your RISE journey.  
  • The system transition workbench is provided for the purpose of streamlining and standardizing the transition paths to RISE. 
  • It is a combination of manual and automated step-by-step instructions organized in Cockpits to complete the process successfully. 
  • It brings together existing tooling as required for the selected transition approach. 
  • It helps with preparation and prerequisite activities, ensuring system readiness. 
  • It provides growing set of health and quality checks reflecting SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS)'s standards for a smooth handover and reduced downtime. 

Available Approaches 
The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench supports different ways for migrating systems. Each method comes with detailed documentation that explains the process. The documentation gives clear steps and guidance to handle specific migration needs. 

 Refer to SAP Help: Cockpits Supported by "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" 

 

Cockpits Supported by "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" 

  • SAP HANA Tenant Copy 

  • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery 

  • Homogeneous "Update and Copy" 

  • System Conversion: SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move) , SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) 

 How the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" works  
With RISE for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, operating system (OS) access is typically restricted. During the transition to RISE, a virtual machine is provided to perform some tasks which requires OS-level access. We call it a Migration Virtual Machine (VM). The Migration VM allows you to create a temporary system (intermediate System) and perform your chosen transition approach. 

The "System Transition Workbench" runs on the Migration VM. 

The System Transition Workbench automatically downloads the required Software Logistics (SL) tools from the Software Download Service (SDS). 

It makes sure that the latest version of each tool is used and integrates them to work together during the process. 

The "System Transition Workbench" stores all created and necessary secrets in a password vault. For more information, see Security Features. 

During the preparation phase the "System Transition Workbench" requires an internet connection to install a temporary system (intermediate System) on the Migration VM.  

The PAS (primary application server) instance cannot start since it is not connected to a database yet. It is required for the postprocessing steps; there it gets started and connected to the target database to perform the final checks. A direct connection is required between the source and target database. 

Preparation activities on SAP ECS and customer side 

Preparatory actions to be performed by SAP ECS 

  • The SAP system transition workbench is installed by SAP ECS on a Migration VM. Once the Migration VM is ready, SAP ECS hands it over to the migration partner. 
  • Before installing the System Transition Workbench, the SAP Host Agent must be set up correctly with the right certificates. SAP ECS handles this setup. 

Note: Scenarios in the Early Adopter Care program are by default not included on the Migration VM. If you are part of such an Early Adopter Care program, you must ask SAP ECS to enable it on the VM. 

Actions to be performed by users 

  • The workbench requires access to the Internet, preconfigured by the user's proxy. 
  • Remote Function Call (RFC) 

 How to Access the "RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench" to Run Cockpits 

Access the https://<Migration VM name>:1129/lmsl/slocon/docs/index.html with which you can use the same <Migration VM name> for several procedures. Authenticate using the sapadm administrator user of the SAP Host Agent. 

Once you have logged in, you can see System Transition Workbench catalog with the available Cockpits. 

To select the required Cockpit, choose the Create button and follow the instructions provided. 

Advantages  

  • Smooth, secure fast transition into a cloud model.  
  • Safeguarded transition through step-by-step guidance covering all necessary steps, based on SAP best practices. 
  • Resilient transition through automation, reducing the risk of errors and allowing predictability of the execution. 
  • Ensured resulting system quality, measured via a growing set of health and quality checks relevant for all systems. 
  • Simplifies and clarifies migration approaches to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. 

Usage Restriction: 

  • Above mentioned scenario are permitted for SAP ECS internal operations as well as for qualified migration partners and SAP Services. 
  • Qualified migration partners must have earned at least the “Essential Level” designation for the  SAP Cloud ERP Private competency as visible in the SAP Partner Finder. 
  • SAP provides required automation tooling and configuration on the virtual machine for the time of migration. 
  • Any non-authorized software export of the automation tooling or its configuration is prohibited. 
  • Any usage beyond defined usage constraints is prohibited. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench assumes that the target system and component releases are under maintenance. Technical limitations and/or (general) restrictions might require additional preparation or an upgrade to newer releases of SAP S/4HANA related applications, add-ons, or other technical components. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench is currently not available for SAP National Security Services (SAP NS2) customers. 
  • Currently, the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench does not support SAP HANA scale-out target systems. 
  • The System Conversion using 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) for SAP ECC system with MSSQL databases is currently not supported. 

Frequently asked questions: 

  • How to check the version of the SAP System Transition Workbench? 

Log in to the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench with URL https://<Migration VM name>:1129/lmsl/slocon/docs/index.html.  

Click the "About" button in the top right corner of the UI.  

Always verify the latest version in the tool. Refer to SAP Note 3462243 - RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench .

 

  • Does the System Transition Workbench" require Internet access?  

Yes, it requires internet access. Make sure that the user's proxy settings have been correctly configured. The SAP ECS proxy can be used for this purpose. 

 

  • Can I run multiple migrations simultaneously using a single Migration VM?  

No, you cannot run multiple migrations simultaneously. 

 

  • How to request/update thee "System Transition Workbench"? 

Go to SAP For Me , Services and Support dashboard -->Service Catalogue -->open the service request Update Transition Workbench. 

You can request a fresh installation, upgrade to the latest version, or re-installation of the RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench on the migration server. 

 References : 

https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/rise-with-sap-system-transition-workbench.html?anchorId=section_867077864 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/cc75825f2d7049db96b6c60ec269aac6.html?version=SHIP 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3462243 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLTOOLSET/940e4b48bd434bcb95db32940d08694f/1276a251b9e2402f8b1f644bea60c768.html?version=SHIP 

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transformation/btc/stw.html

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team

entry.title: Reuse a DTV (Data Transition Validation) Project in a New BTC Cycle
entry.content:
Overview

When you create a new cycle in the Manage Cycles app, the system automatically creates a new DTV project. In this situation, you may want to reuse the settings from an existing project.

Prerequisites

You created a new cycle in the Manage Cycles app and set the Run Data Transition Validation (DTV) Tool option to Yes.

Procedure

  1. In transaction DTV, export the project that has the settings you want to reuse.

  2. The system automatically creates a new DTV project when you create the cycle. In transaction DTV, locate this project and copy the project ID. This ID will be required in step 4.

    Note: The project ID is the same as the cycle ID that you can view in the Manage Cycles app.

  3. Change the project ID of the newly created project to a temporary name.

  4. Restart transaction DTV and import the project you exported in step 1, but specify the project ID you copied in step 2. This creates a new project with the project ID of your new cycle and that contains the configuration settings you want to reuse.

  5. (Optional) Delete the project you renamed in step 3.

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Implementing an editable tree view (hierarchy)
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.
This blog explains how to expose the hierarchical structure of a CDS hierarchy as an editable tree view in the CUBCO app.
For this purpose the SMBC object now supports tree table as table type.

For more details on hierarchies and more detailed examples and explanations, see the links above. The purpose of this blog is to demonstrate the tree view capability of the CUBCO app using a simple example.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (2025, SAP Note 3687833, SAP UI5 1.136.12)
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment (2605)

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored in a parent-child relationship to organize the corporate structure in a recursive hierarchy.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  parent_company        : abap.numc(4);
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Implement editable tree view

First we use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object for this table. In the wizard, select the option "Create Projection Layer". Apart from this, leave settings as they are and complete the wizard.

Add recursive associations

Add associations _ParentCompany and _Company to generated CDS view ZI_COMPANYCODE. Save and activate.

@EndUserText.label: 'Company Code'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #MANDATORY
define view entity ZI_CompanyCode
  as select from zdemo_cc
  association         to parent ZI_CompanyCode_S as _CompanyCodeAll on $projection.SingletonID = _CompanyCodeAll.SingletonID
  association of many to one ZI_CompanyCode      as _ParentCompany  on $projection.ParentCompany = _ParentCompany.CompanyCode
  association of many to many ZI_CompanyCode     as _Company        on $projection.CompanyCode = _Company.ParentCompany

{
  key company_code          as CompanyCode,
      @Semantics.address.country: true
      country               as Country,
      parent_company        as ParentCompany,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,
      1                     as SingletonID,
      _CompanyCodeAll,
      _ParentCompany,
      _Company
}

Create CDS hierarchy

Create a new CDS Data Definition ZI_COMPANYCODEHN. Save and activate.

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
define hierarchy ZI_COMPANYCODEHN
  with parameters
    P_SingletonID : abap.int1
  as parent child hierarchy(
    source ZI_CompanyCode
    child to parent association _ParentCompany
    directory _CompanyCodeAll filter by
      SingletonID = $parameters.P_SingletonID
    start where
      ParentCompany is initial
    siblings order by
      CompanyCode ascending
  )
{
  key CompanyCode,
      ParentCompany,
      SingletonID
}

Add recursive hierarchy to CDS definition

Add the recursive hierarchy and the redirections to the generated projection CDS view ZC_COMPANYCODE

@EndUserText.label: 'Maintain Company Code'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #MANDATORY
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@OData.hierarchy.recursiveHierarchy:[{ entity.name: 'ZI_COMPANYCODEHN' }]
define view entity ZC_CompanyCode
  as projection on ZI_CompanyCode
{
  key CompanyCode,
      Country,
      ParentCompany,
      LastChangedAt,
      @Consumption.hidden: true
      LocalLastChangedAt,
      @Consumption.hidden: true
      SingletonID,
      _CompanyCodeAll : redirected to parent ZC_CompanyCode_S,
      _ParentCompany  : redirected to ZC_CompanyCode,
      _Company        : redirected to ZC_CompanyCode
}

Adjust behavior definition

Add the following to the definition of entity CompanyCode in the generated behavior definition ZI_COMPANYCODE_S. Save and activate.

  association _ParentCompany
  {
    with draft;
    link action linkCompany;
    unlink action unlinkCompany;
  }

  association _Company { with draft; }

  instance hierarchy ZI_COMPANYCODEHN
  {
    ascending association _ParentCompany;
    descending association _Company { with cascading delete; }
  }

Extend the behavior projection ZC_COMPANYCODE_S accordingly. Save and activate.

define behavior for ZC_CompanyCode alias CompanyCode
use etag
{
  use update;
  use delete;

  use association _CompanyCodeAll { with draft; }
  use association _ParentCompany
  {
    with draft;
    link action linkManager;
    unlink action unlinkManager;
  }
  use association _Company { with draft; }

}

Set table type

Open the generated SMBC object in ADT. Expand the Table Settings section, select the row for entity CompanyCode and click Edit.
Change Table Type to Tree Table and Table Creation Mode to New Page . Save and activate.

Result

You can maintain your company structure while using the hierarchy features of SAP Fiori elements, such as: Collapse/expand tree, cut and paste nodes, or drag and drop nodes.

Further considerations

  • You can set the ParentCompany field as read-only. The user can still change the parent company, for example, using drag and drop functionality. However, you cannot modify the ParentCompany field with ABAP EML, only with ABAP SQL.
  • You can remove the ParentCompany field from the tree table by removing the corresponding UI.lineItem from the metadata extension. It should be sufficient to display the field on the object page as the parent is specified by the visible hierarchy structure.
  • If the ParentCompany field is enabled for editing, you can provide a value help and implement a validation for create and update to prevent an inconsistent hierarchy. However, a validation does not prevent the update of the draft version. When you reload the app, the entity with an invalid parent disappears. Deletion is configured as cascading, so no additional validation is required for the deletion process. 
  • Instead, consider using a precheck to prevent inconsistent hierarchy changes.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP Private 2025 Key Innovations in Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP Private 2025 Key Innovations in Sourcing and Procurement

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 release for Sourcing and Procurement introduces several valuable enhancements, most notably the new SAP Fiori app “My Purchase Requisitions – New.” This blog provides a concise overview of the new capabilities.

1) Start and End Dates for Service Items

Service items (product type Service) now include Start Date and End Date fields to define the service period, replacing the single Delivery Date field.

This feature enables accurate representation of services as Services are delivered over a period but not on one day. Capturing Start/End Date aligns the requisition with how services are actually performed and consumed.

This feature also allows Improved compliance and control and specifying explicit service periods support policy compliance (e.g., contract validity, budget availability for the period, approval rules tied to dates) and reduce the risk of ordering services outside approved timeframes.

2) Harmonized Document Management (HDM) in “Create Purchase Order – Advanced”

The Create Purchase Order – Advanced app now supports Harmonized Document Management (HDM) for purchase order header-level attachments.

With this release, Harmonized Document Management (HDM) can be used for attachments at purchase order header level. This is SAP’s unified approach to have all the documents at one place instead of several places and across all Fiori apps.

This Fiori app Create Purchase Order-Advanced now allows to attach the documents at header level, as it aligns with SAP’s unified approach to have all documents at one place instead of several places and across all Fiori apps.

3) Aggregation of Lean Service–Related PO History Items

Lean service standard items and enhanced limit items can now be included in ME87 (Summarization and Removal of PO History Records). 

For SAP Lean Services, aggregating Lean Service–related PO History items (e.g., for Service POs tied to SES) creates value mainly by improving control, speed, and transparency across PO → SES → GR/IR → Invoice.

It also delivers other business values in End-to-End visibility for services spend, faster SES and Invoice exception handling, Better Audit trail and compliance are some of them.

However, there are certain restrictions on using the RM06EKBE report:

  • Parked invoices will not be included in the aggregation.
  • Blocked invoices will not be included in the aggregation.
  • Aggregation will not occur if the GR quantity and invoice quantity don’t match.
  • Unapproved SES will not be aggregated.

4) Display Header Attachments for Purchase Requisition Items in “My Inbox”

Approvers using the My Inbox app can now view purchase requisition header-level attachments (from the GOS table) directly when approving PR items—alongside item-level attachments (from the DMS table).

If you approve a purchase requisition item using the My Inbox app, you can now directly display the attachments on header level (located in the GOS table) in addition to the attachments on item level (located in the DMS table) instead of navigating to the header as before.

5) Add PR Items from a Model Product Specification (MPS)

With this Fiori app Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional, you can now use the Items/Item Sets from MPS option under Create to select and add items or items from an MPS. This option is only available if the configuration has activated the item hierarchy function and you have switched on the Hierarchy List view in the purchase requisition items table.

This feature enables quicker PR creation as Users can generate a complete, multi-line requisition in seconds instead of manually entering items, quantities, and attributes instead of lines by line.

It also reduces cycle time (requisition lead time) from request to approval and purchasing.

  • On selecting an MPS ID and choosing OK, the items and item sets in that MPS are listed. You can then select the items and item sets that you want to add to the item table and choose Add.

  • The selected items and item sets are now added to the purchase requisition items table.

Effects on Configuration

In the standard system, the item hierarchy function is switched off by default. The configuration expert can activate the item hierarchy function using the configuration step Activate Item Hierarchies in Purchasing present under Sourcing and Procurement -->General Settings -->Item Hierarchy. Note that once the item hierarchy function is switched on, it cannot be turned off.

The new features can be accessed from below SAP Help site.

https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5fc51e30e2744f168642e26e0c1d9be1?clear=all&Version=2025&Line_of_Business=Sourcing+and+Procurement&Business_Area=Operational+Procurement

Thank you for reading, and I encourage everyone to embrace the latest innovations that come with SAP S/4HANA!. If you find this blog useful, please consider giving it a "like" or sharing it. I’d love to hear your thoughts and feedback, so feel free to leave a comment. If you have any questions, just drop them in the Q&A section with the tags SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We'll try to get back to you as soon as we can.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Enable associated entities as a read-only navigation target
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

Business software is a tightly integrated network. Application data, configuration data and system data form a giant relational network.
This blog explains how to enable associated entities as a read-only navigation target in the CUBCO app. This can be used, for example, to integrate a where-used list into the configuration app to visualize part of the relational network.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP BTP ABAP environment (2605)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (2025, SAP Note 3694141, SAP UI5 1.136.12)

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In this simplified scenario, there is one configuration table for company codes and one configuration table for plants. Each plant is assigned to a company code. Our goal is to display on the object page of a company code all plants to which this company code is assigned. For both tables we use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : zdemo_cc_id not null;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}
@EndUserText.label : 'Plant'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_plant {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key plant_id          : abap.numc(3) not null;
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.keyType : #KEY
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : true
  company_code          : zdemo_cc_id
    with foreign key zdemo_cc
      where client = zdemo_plant.client
        and company_code = zdemo_plant.company_code;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Create read-only navigation target

Create a new projection view entity for entity plant without a behavior definition so the associated entities cannot be edited. Do not define associations to other editable RAP BOs to keep draft, transport, and concurrency handling separate and correct.

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Plant'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view entity ZC_PLANT
  as select from ZI_Plant
{
      .identification: [ {
        position: 1
      } ]
      .lineItem: [ {
        position: 1
      } ]
      .facet: [ {
        id: 'Plant',
        purpose: #STANDARD,
        type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
        label: 'Plant',
        position: 1
      } ]
  key PlantId,
      CompanyCode,
      .identification: [ {
      position: 2
      } ]
      .lineItem: [ {
        position: 2
      } ]
      Country
}

Access Control:

@EndUserText.label: 'Mapping Role for ZC_Plant'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZC_PLANT {
  grant
    select
      on
        ZC_PLANT
          where
            inheriting conditions from entity ZI_Plant;           
}

Add the association to the company code entity:

@EndUserText.label: 'Company Code'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #MANDATORY
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view entity ZI_CompanyCode
  as select from zdemo_cc
  association to parent ZI_CompanyCode_S as _CompanyCodeAll on $projection.SingletonID = _CompanyCodeAll.SingletonID
  association of many to many ZC_PLANT as _Plant on $projection.CompanyCode = _Plant.CompanyCode
{
  key company_code as CompanyCode,
  @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
  last_changed_at as LastChangedAt,
  @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
  @Consumption.hidden: true
  local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,
  @Consumption.hidden: true
  1 as SingletonID,
  _CompanyCodeAll,
  _Plant
}

Add a new facet to the metadata extension of company code:

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
@UI: {
  headerInfo: {
    typeName: 'CompanyCode', 
    typeNamePlural: 'CompanyCodes', 
    title: {
      type: #STANDARD, 
      label: 'Company Code', 
      value: 'CompanyCode'
    }
  }
}
annotate view ZI_CompanyCode with
{
  .identification: [ {
    position: 1 
  } ]
  .lineItem: [ {
    position: 1 
  } ]
  .facet: [ {
    id: 'CompanyCode', 
    purpose: #STANDARD, 
    type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE, 
    label: 'Company Code', 
    position: 1 
  },
  {
    id: 'Plant', 
    type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE, 
    label: 'Plant', 
    position: 2 , 
    targetElement: '_Plant'
  } ]
  CompanyCode;
}

Add the plant entity to the service definition of company code:

@ObjectModel.leadingEntity.name: 'ZI_CompanyCode_S'
define service ZUI_COMPANYCODE {
  expose ZI_CompanyCode_S as CompanyCodeAll;
  expose ZI_CompanyCode   as CompanyCode;
  expose ZC_PLANT         as Plant;
}

To enable the user to navigate from the object page of a company code to the object page of a plant, add a row for the plant entity in the "Table Settings" section of the BC maintenance object of the company code.

The company code object page now displays all plants that are assigned to this company code. While the user can edit the company code, the list of plants is not editable.

Intent navigation

The next step to improve the application is to annotate the Plant field with an intent navigation to the Custom Business Configurations app. This allows the user to navigate from the company code object page to the maintenance app of a specific plant.

Currently, this requires the creation of a custom navigation target as it is not possible to use a constant in the semanticObjectBinding annotation. 

If you think this type of intent navigation is important to your application, you can vote and comment on this improvement request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/338756/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Third-Party Payments with SIPO
entry.content:
This blog post introduces business scenarios and explains how to process payment with SIPO in Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition. 

SIPO (Soustředěné Inkaso Plateb Obyvatelstva) that is used for direct debit of multiple payments through the Czech Post’s system. It is a national payment aggregation system operated by the Czech Post. This business concept relates to payments through a third party. 

Business Scenarios

Let’s start with the parties involved in SIPO payments.  

  1. Customers that have SIPO connection number pay on a regular basis 
  2. Service providers that offer services of utilities, multimedia, or internet, send payment requests to SIPO 
  3. Czech Post Office, or the SIPO operator, collects all payment requests, aggregates them into one payment, and distribute paid money by customers to service providers 
  4. Banks that serve as payment channels carry out direct debit from bank accounts

There are two business scenarios of payments involving SIPO: 

Scenario 1: You, as a service provider, post receivable items. After executing a payment run, you send a SIPO file, as an outgoing file, to the Czech Post for informing the customers to pay.

Scenario 2: Continuing with scenario 1, you, as a service provider, want to clear the open items by processing incoming SIPO files after your customer completes the payment. 

Prerequisites

Bank Account Settings

Since the payments with SIPO order are not directly paid out from the payer's bank account, instead it is paid out from the post office, it is necessary to create a fictive bank account and account ID for the post office.

Open the Manage Bank Accounts app.  
For your company code, create a fictive Bank Account and Account ID for the Czech Post. 

Define Parameters for Paying Out

Since payment is carried out through the third party (Czech Post),  you need to identify which payee whom the post office pays.   

Use the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions view (TFK012D) in the transaction SM30 to fill the service provider's SIPO number in the Customer number field.

Define Payment Methods 

Use the Define Payment Methods (ID: 101952) View cluster VC_TFK042Z in the transaction SM34. 

Create a payment method or use the pre-delivered format - CZ_PSTLPAYMENT_SIPO as the payment medium format for Czech SIPO payments.

Define Payment Lot Specifications

Use the Define Payment Lot Specification view ( V_TFKIDEXPC_ZK ) in the transaction SM30. 

Select the format type Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) and complete the Bank statement settings section.  
Note: It is not required to configure the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-PZ). 

Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee

Use  the Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee view cluster (VC_TFK006) to assign keywords.

Procedure

  1. You post contract accounting receivable items. 
  2. Execute a payment run. The payment order then is created. 
  3. During the payment run, the system uses the payment format CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-SIPO to generate the payment file for the Czech Post. 
  4. You then send the payment file to Czech Post. 
    Czech Post informs the customer to conduct the payment.

Processing SIPO Incoming Payment Files 

After the customer pays, the Czech Post sends back the SIPO files.  

The SIPO files contain the following files:

  • SIPO header file – Includes the total settlement amount that matches the bank statement of the incoming payment 
  • SIPO item file – lists the clearing of open receivable items per customer’s individual SIPO number 

You have to import both files in the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680).

  1. Open the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680). 
    • Select Import and choose Third-party Payment.
    • Select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) and upload the SIPO header file.
    • Repeat the same operation and upload the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file).

  2. Open the Process Imported Third-Party Payments app (F9000) to schedule a run. 
    • Select Third-Party Payment: Transfer data as the job template.  
    • In the Parameters, select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) as a format type.  
      Note: When processing the imported file, you only need to process the item files. (Header files are not required.)  
    • Schedule a run and you can see that the payment lot number is created in the log.

      If you select the Post Lot check box and you can directly process the payment order lot without additional operation.

  3. Use the payment lot and process it in the Process Payment (Order) Lot transaction (FP45) and clear open items of the customer. If you did not select the checkbox in the previous step.

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: Q1 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Register for our Q1 2026 Service Solutions webcast

2026 will prove to be a transformational year for many companies as AI assistants and agents drive high value in service delivery. I’m already excited about the Q2 Innovations blog! Get registered and ready for a preview at Sapphire 2026 of how SAP’s embedded AI innovations are delivering value for customers every day. But first things first, let me highlight some key innovations delivered in Q1 for SAP Enterprise Service Management (SAP ESM).

Ensuring seamless look and feel throughout a unified workspace

Delivering the right user experience has high impact on value and adoption for service specialists especially when the ESM workspace includes third party applications and processes. Using mashups is the preferred method to create an integrated environment ensuring service representatives don’t have to login to different systems or open sperate windows. Now the configuration for mashups has been extended to include theme parameters. With this setting the embedded mashup will adjust automatically to the system set theme providing a consistent look and feel for SAP and 3rd party embedded solutions.

Deliver connected experiences and activity across solutions

The event hub service in BTP allows solutions to publish inbound and outbound events ensuring others can access these activities for visibility and execution of line of business specific processes. SAP ESM now publishes outbound events to the SAP BTP Cloud Application Event Hub so other applications  receive them ensuring a unified and transparent experience across systems. This simplifies the need for system-to-system integration and centralizes events. This transparency can be realized with SAP SuccessFactors ensuring case updates are provided on employee service interactions directly on the SuccessFactors home page.

Self-Service available anytime, anywhere

Employees can get instant answers and create cases from the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app providing easy 24/7 access for HR support. This provides greater flexibility and ease of access for HR related inquiries and gives employees the ability to work from their channel of choice, even if they are on the go. HR Case management is available as well, allowing creation of new cases, viewing and updating existing cases from wherever they are. Additionally, employees receive notifications when action is needed, ensuring case resolution without response delays.

Accelerating case resolution with UX simplicity

An approval process can delay case resolutions. Working with our customers’ inputs, several improvements have been made that reduce time, ensure consistency and enable a more streamlined handling of cases where approvals are needed. Ensuring quick steps to final resolution, both Service reps and managers benefit from a more efficient process and consistency while approvals are in progress. The following capabilities have been enabled to accelerate the approval process:

  • Approval steps enabled in auto flow scenarios
  • Case status triggers notifications or email
  • Directly approve from case header
  • Easy access to visibility in next steps and status
  • Read-only case state during approval steps


Flexing AI to accelerate case resolution

Another key improvement in Q1 includes extending the business information extraction capability to include custom fields. This capability saves time, reduces errors and delivers ease to service teams – eliminating the need to scour through email for relevant information and then having to make several manual entries. SAP to SAP solutions is rather logical, but often there are custom fields or 3rd party solutions. Now with custom fields included there’s additional flexibility ensuring service reps have improved automation, prepossessing and context to quickly close cases.

 
 

 

More Information & Thank you!

For additional Q1 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management, reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q1 Release Webinar, and to keep up as we release capabilities each month reference the What’s New in our help portal. Please also reference these valuable links for more on SAP’s CX Customer Service Solutions:

SAP learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Innovation Spotlight Webinar

Release Navigator

To learn more about our Q1/2026 innovations across SAP’s CX solutions check out the blog!

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics!

Finally, an enormous thank you to this expanding community, for your active participation and collaboration; as we all benefit – SAP employees, partners and customers -  from sharing our experiences and information on SAP ESM.

entry.title: New SAP Signavio Value Accelerators: Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
We are excited to announce two new additions to the SAP Signavio Value Accelerator Library:

  • Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Process Landscape Design & Fit-to-Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

These accelerators bring a harmonized process structure that connects the SAP Reference Business Process Architecture with SAP S/4HANA best practices ready to use out of the box. They are built for everyone working on transformation, running Fit-to-Standard workshops, or who’s simply looking to enrich their Signavio workspace with first process content to get started.

Key highlights include:

  • SAP Reference Business Process Architecture including specific end-to-end variants
  • Out-of-the-box synchronization with SAP LeanIX thanks to renewed structure
  • Mapping to S/4HANA best practice diagrams
  • Dictionary entries to capture information structurally

You can find both accelerators directly in your SAP Signavio workspace by opening the Value Accelerator Library (VAL) and searching for "Process Landscape Design".

We have put together more information and a step-by-step installation and setup guide. Check it out here.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to your BTM contact.

entry.title: SAP CX Product Inspiration Series 2026: Execute CX as your Growth Stratetegy
entry.content:
Customer experience has entered a new era. In 2026, engagement alone is no longer enough. Businesses win by executing with intelligence, speed, and context across the entire enterprise. With AI embedded directly into sales, service, commerce, and engagement workflows, SAP Customer Experience is transforming CX from a front-office function into a true growth engine of your company. In this new Product Inspiration Series, we explore how SAP CX brings executional AI into the flow of work by connecting customer signals to real outcomes, powered by the business data and processes that run your organization.
The special character of the SAP CX Product Inspiration Series remains unchanged. Each video:
  • spotlights concrete innovations across the SAP CX portfolio
  • is created by product and industry experts, for fellow experts
  • opens with a short Q&A to set the context—the why, what, and how
  • follows a zero‑slides approach, focusing entirely on the product itself
  • delivers in‑depth explorations and hands‑on demos
  • helps you better understand the Consumer Industries and SAP CX product strategy
  • is crafted with passion and dedication for you—our customers, partners, colleagues, and CX enthusiasts

Now, let’s take a closer look at the first Product Inspiration videos that have crossed the finish line…

1. Gainsight Customer Success Management for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

 

Join Emma Falldorf, Product Manager SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 at SAP, for an exciting overview of the latest integrations between Gainsight Customer Success Management and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. In this insightful video, we dive deep into the key benefits and usage scenarios of this powerful integration, provide a comprehensive look at how Gainsight enhances the customer experience.

2. Account Planning in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 in Action

Join Crystal Zhang, Product Manager SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 at SAP, for an exciting overview of the latest Account Planning capabilities of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2.
In this SAP Product Inspiration Video, learn why intelligent account planning is essential for modern sales teams and how SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 brings structure, insight, and focus to key account management. Through a concise end-to-end demo, the video shows how sales teams can capture customer goals and insights, define strategies and sales plans, monitor pipeline health, collaborate through action plans, and generate AI-powered executive summaries—all within one integrated solution. Discover how Intelligent CX from SAP helps turn account insights into consistent, measurable sales success.

The key highlights shared in this video are:

3. AI Scouting in SAP Sports One

Join Michael Kesselmann, Product Manager SAP Sports One, and Achim Ittner, VP for Sports at SAP, for an exciting overview on embedded AI scouting capabilities in SAP Sports One. In this video, you will learn how clubs and federations can leverage Generative AI to streamline scouting workflows, accelerate decision-making, and create high‑quality player summaries with ease.
Discover how SAP Joule enhances the scouting process by consolidating extensive written reports, answering performance-related questions instantly, and generating structured management summaries that support faster and more informed squad planning decisions.

entry.title: Activating Joule for Developers (J4D) – BTP Trust Store Certificate Configuration in STRUST
entry.content:
This blog is a continuation of the series on activating Joule for Developers (J4D) with ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025. It focuses specifically on the BTP Trust Store Certificate installation step — uploading the required .pem certificates into the SSL Client created during the destination certificate setup.

Prerequisites

Before following the steps in this blog, ensure you have completed the destination certificate configuration described in the blog below. A new SSL Client (e.g., J4DFIN) must already be created with the destination certificate in place.

  • Destination certificate configured and SSL Client created — Generate Client Certificate for Joule for Developers: ABAP AI Capabilities
  • Access to transaction code STRUST (Trust Manager) in the SAP system
  • Access to transaction code SMICM (ICM Monitor) for the soft restart
  • All required .pem certificates downloaded from the BTP Trust Store repository on GitHub

Background

For Joule for Developers to communicate securely with SAP BTP services, the SAP system must trust the certificate authorities used by BTP. These root and intermediate CA certificates are maintained in the official SAP BTP Trust Store repository on GitHub:

https://github.com/sap-software/btp-trust-store/tree/main/required/pem

All certificates listed under the /required/pem path must be imported into the SSL Client in STRUST so that outbound TLS connections from the ABAP system to BTP endpoints are established successfully.

Step-by-Step: Installing BTP Trust Store Certificates

Step 1 — Download the Required Certificates

Navigate to the BTP Trust Store GitHub repository and download all .pem certificate files from the /required/pem folder. These include DigiCert, SAP, Entrust, and ISRG root certificates.

Step 2 — Open STRUST and Locate the SSL Client

Open transaction STRUST in the SAP system. In the navigation tree on the left, locate and double-click the SSL Client that was created in the prerequisite step (e.g., SSL client J4D forABAP Test Subaccount or J4DFIN). The panel on the right will display the Own Certificate section, confirming the destination certificate is in place.

Figure 1: Trust Manager (STRUST) – SSL Client with Own Certificate (destination certificate) already configured

Step 3 — Import Each Certificate
